findings.text: Module docstring said 'Gaussian Process regression with an RBF + White kernel' — but the code runs a full three-family CV (GP, spline, KRR) and the winning model is KernelRidge(gamma=0.25, alpha=1e-3). ADDRESSED: docstring updated in autorun round-2 polish commit.
CV grid runs at module import time, so the full 35-candidate grid evaluates once per seed invocation. Not a correctness issue — deterministic — but an efficiency note.
No test-set leakage detected. solution.py reads only research/eval/data_train.npz; evaluator's hidden target and test x-grid are not referenced anywhere.

All hyperparameters in the code match the log: KFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=0), gamma_grid includes 0.25, alpha_grid includes 1e-3, spline_grid matches, GP uses n_restarts_optimizer=4 and normalize_y=True.
Single-panel figure; narrative.png is a secondary figure (not results.png) so this is not a hard violation, but duplicates content from results.png panel (a).
Panel (c) sigma^2 noise-floor annotation is easy to miss at 150 DPI; consider larger font or a vertical reference line.

findings.issue: Undefined abbreviations: 'CV', 'MSE', 'RBF' not expanded on first use.
findings.fix: Expand on first use, or add a brief ## Glossary.

findings.text: suspicious_improvement=true is triggered by 99.98% vs baseline but entirely explained by context: the constant-mean baseline is a trivially weak predictor (MSE=1.42). Log proactively documents the ~4700x figure and the sigma^2 floor argument. No hype language.
All three seeds return identical MSE (0.000299) because the evaluator's test x-grid is seed-independent by construction.

synopsis: Orbit 01-smooth-regressor applies 5-fold CV over a GP/spline/KRR family to the 200-point noisy training set, selecting KernelRidge(gamma=0.25, alpha=1e-3) with test MSE=2.99e-4 — 33x below target and fully deterministic across seeds. Significant and well-explained; the only substantive code finding (stale module docstring) was patched in round-2 polish. Prose and figures are strong; minor items are three unexpanded acronyms and a redundant single-panel narrative.png that duplicates results.png panel (a).
